perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be instead of all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall not be limited to direct damages but shall include any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Such remedy shall not be the exclusive remedy' to 'Such remedy shall be the exclusive remedy' and changing 'shall be limite d to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability' to 'shall not be limited to direct damages but shall include any other liability' directly contradicts the original intent of the Remedies section. This amendment now makes injunctive relief the sole remedy and encompasses liability for special, indirect, punitive, or consequential damages, which was originally excluded, thus changing the remedy constraints of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or prevent any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be instead of all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, the Receiving Party’s liability to the Disclosing Party in connection with this Agreement and any activities undertaken in connection with the evaluation of the Transactions shall not be limited to direct damages but shall include any other liability, including without limitation liability for special, indirect, punitive or consequential damages in contract, tort, warranty, strict liability or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted section. The original text states remedies are *not* exclusive and limits liability to direct damages. The changed text makes injunctive relief the *exclusive* remedy and includes liability for all damages, which creates a direct conflict and incompatibility between the original contractual terms and the modified terms. The 'Perturbation Explanation' clearly states this contradiction, and by changing 'not the exclusive remedy' to 'the exclusive remedy' and 'limited to direct damages and shall exclude any other liability' to 'shall not be limited to direct damages but shall include any other liability' a clear and direct conflict is created. The definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction applies here, given that a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly fol lowing any decision by the Receiving Party not to continue discussions with respect to the Transactions , and at any other time upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy, at the Receiving Party’s option, all wr itten Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including that portion of such Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by, or for, the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party and it s Agents shall not retain any copies of such written Confidential Information. At any time after which the Receiving Party has been required to return or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession in accordance with the preceding sentence, the Receiving Party shall, upon written request of the Disclosing Party, cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that the requirements of the preceding sentence have Signal NDCA 20160308 3 been satisfied in full. The Receiving Party shall not be deemed to have retained or failed to destroy any Confidential Information which is an Imaged Document if such Confidential Information is deleted from local hard drives so long as no attempt is made to recover such Confidential Information fro m servers or back -up sources, provided that any such retained Confidential Information in an Imaged Document form shall remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly fol lowing any decision by the Receiving Party not to continue discussions with respect to the Transactions , the Receiving Party may retain, at the Receiving Party’s option, all wr itten Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including that portion of such Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by, or for, the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party and it s Agents may retain any copies of such written Confidential Information. At any time after which the Receiving Party has decided to retain the Confidential Information in its possession in accordance with the preceding sentence, the Receiving Party shall not cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that the requirements of the preceding sentence have Signal NDCA 20160308 3 been satisfied in full. The Receiving Party shall be deemed to have retained or failed to destroy any Confidential Information which is an Imaged Document if such Confidential Information remains on local hard drives without needing to be deleted nor will an attempt to recover such Confidential Information from servers or back -up sources, provided that any such retained Confidential Information in an Imaged Document form shall not remain subject to the disclosure and use restrictions set forth herein, notwithstanding any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 5 to allow the Receiving Party to retain Confidential Information after discussions end and to not require certification of destruction, and additionally stating imaged documents don't need to be deleted and won't be subject to disclosure and use restrictions, directly contradicts the core purpose of the NDA which is to protect the Confidential Information. This now conflicts with section 2 which requires strict confidentiality. The change fundamentally undermines the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be used for any purpose other than to analyze, implement or complet e the Transactions . Confidential Information shall be held in strict confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be disclosed without prior written consent of Disclosing Party, except to those advisors, affiliates, agents, assigns, attorneys, employees, d irectors, officers and/or members (“Agents”) with a need -to-know the Confidential Information for the purposes of analyzing, implementing or completing the Transactions . Receiving Party shall require all recipients of the Confidential Information to be bo und by the terms of this Agreement. Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Agents. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party employs to protect its own information of like importance, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care based on industry standard.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 directly contradicts Section 2 (Nondisclosure and Use of Confidential Information). The original text required the Receiving Party to return or destroy confidential information. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain confidential information and removes the requirement for certification of destruction. Furthermore, it states that imaged documents need not be deleted and will not be subject to disclosure and use restrictions. This directly conflicts with Section 2, which mandates strict confidentiality and prohibits the use or disclosure of confidential information without prior written consent, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction as it undermines the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate three (3 ) year s following written notice by either Party to the other of its desire to terminate th is Agreement. However, the obligations contained herein shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the date the Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate three (3 ) year s following written notice by either Party to the other of its desire to terminate th is Agreement. However, the obligations contained herein shall remain in effect indefinitely from the date the Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term of the confidentiality obligations from five years to indefinitely creates a contradiction within Section 13. The first sentence establishes a termination date for the entire agreement, while the second now states that the confidentiality obligations last forever. This is an internal contradiction that introduces uncertainty about the duration of the NDA's key obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement shall terminate three (3 ) year s following written notice by either Party to the other of its desire to terminate th is Agreement. However, the obligations contained herein shall remain in effect indefinitely from the date the Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the duration of the confidentiality obligations from a defined period (five years) to 'indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the first sentence in both the changed text and the contradicted text, which states that the agreement terminates after three years following written notice. The contradiction lies in the fact that the agreement is supposed to end after a certain period, but a key obligation (confidentiality) now lasts forever, creating an inconsistency and uncertainty about the overall term and enforceability of the agreement as described in the perturbation explanation. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, where conflicting implications arise from placing clauses in multiple locations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the full and ent ire agreement between the Parties regarding the confidentiality of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement between the Parties regarding some of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'full and entire agreement' to 'a partial agreement' contradicts the purpose of having an Entire Agreement clause. The clause is supposed to assert that the document contains all the terms agreed upon, but this revision introduces the possibility that there are other unwritten agreements or understandings regarding confidentiality. This undermines the enforceability and predictability of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement between the Parties regarding some of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement was the 'full and entire' agreement. The changed text modifies this to 'a partial agreement'. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text: 'This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement between the Parties regarding some of the Confidential Information.' This creates a direct contradiction because the intent of an 'entire agreement' clause is to represent the complete understanding of the parties, while a 'partial agreement' suggests other agreements exist, thus undermining the original intent and leading to potential unenforceability and disputes as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation and fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Counterparty acknowledges that, under this Agreement, Company is disclosing Confidential Information to Counterpar ty that is valuable, confidential and proprietary. If said Confidential Information were disclosed by Counterparty to any third party, said disclosure would damage Company and negatively affect its opportunity to work on the Transaction, including during bidding process and contract negotiations. Counterparty agrees that on a case by case basis (when confirmed in writing by both Parties ), the Counterparty shall consult, contract, and negotiate exclusively with Company concerning those designated Transacti ons. On such agreed Transaction s, Counterparty agrees and states that it has not and will not enter discussions with any other entity concerning it providing of any services for those Transaction s. Counterparty may not use or disclose the Confidential Information with any third party regarding the Transaction s or otherwise to the disadvantage of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Counterparty acknowledges that, under this Agreement, Company is disclosing Confidential Information to Counterpar ty that may or may not be valuable, confidential and proprietary. If said Confidential Information were disclosed by Counterparty to any third party, said disclosure may not damage Company nor negatively affect its opportunity to work on the Transaction, including during bidding process and contract negotiations. Counterparty agrees that on a case by case basis (when confirmed in writing unilaterally by Counterparty), the Counterparty may consult, contract, and negotiate exclusively with any entity concerning those designated Transacti ons. On such agreed Transaction s, Counterparty agrees and states that it may or may not enter discussions with any other entity concerning it providing of any services for those Transaction s. Counterparty may use or disclose the Confidential Information with any third party regarding the Transaction s or otherwise to the advantage of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'valuable, confidential and proprietary' to 'may or may not be valuable, confidential and proprietary', 'said disclosure would damage Company' to 'said disclosure may not damage Company', 'when confirmed in writing by both Parties' to 'when confirmed in writing unilaterally by Counterparty', 'has not and will not enter discussions with any other entity' to 'may or may not enter discussions with any other entity', and 'disadvantage of the Company' to 'advantage of the Company', this section completely reverses its original meaning and directly contradicts the entire purpose of the NDA. It suggests the information disclosed may not be valuable or confidential, disclosure may not be harmful, and the Counterparty has no obligation to exclusively negotiate with the Company, rendering the agreement meaningless. This makes the clause internally contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information . Counterparty acknowledges that, under this Agreement, Company is disclosing Confidential Information to Counterpar ty that may or may not be valuable, confidential and proprietary. If said Confidential Information were disclosed by Counterparty to any third party, said disclosure may not damage Company nor negatively affect its opportunity to work on the Transaction, including during bidding process and contract negotiations. Counterparty agrees that on a case by case basis (when confirmed in writing unilaterally by Counterparty), the Counterparty may consult, contract, and negotiate exclusively with any entity concerning those designated Transacti ons. On such agreed Transaction s, Counterparty agrees and states that it may or may not enter discussions with any other entity concerning it providing of any services for those Transaction s. Counterparty may use or disclose the Confidential Information with any third party regarding the Transaction s or otherwise to the advantage of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text states that the Confidential Information is 'valuable, confidential and proprietary,' while the changed text states it 'may or may not be valuable, confidential and proprietary.' Similarly, the original states disclosure 'would damage Company,' changed to 'may not damage Company.' The original text requires the Counterparty to negotiate exclusively with the company, which is altered to allow the counterparty to negotiate with any entity, and the original states that Counterparty 'has not and will not enter discussions with any other entity' is changed to 'may or may not enter discussions with any other entity'. These changes directly contradict the fundamental purpose of an NDA, which is to protect confidential information and ensure exclusive negotiation, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text which is identical to the changed text, showing a significant internal consistency conflict.
